comment: Imagine you go to this hotel, your room number is some absurdly high number. It takes hours to get to it, and you're dead tired once you do. You're about ready to throw yourself on the bed when you hear over the PA: "please move to the room number that is 2 times your current number".
I love how mathematicians literally need to invent a problem that doesn't exist

Teacher: don’t ask

This is a pretty involved and important mathematical theory, it's only disguised as a silly "math problem", because most people think that's all there is to maths anyway.. It's fun to picture some cranky mathematician saying to himself: "You know what would be fun? An infinite hotel!" but sadly that's very far from what actually happened.
@tomsvoboda2309  to be fair, Cantor was probably pretty cranky back in the day

​ @tomsvoboda2309  well, strictly speaking, its not THAT important. There are 2 types of mathmatical ideas, those that are critical for society and those that are critical for furthering the knowledge base of mathmatics. Will rhe world collapse if we dont know how infinite sequences will work? Of course not. Now, if we didnt know basic functions like addition, multiplication, etc, then we'd be in trouble.

Some weeks ago I did a test that said: Victor written all number from 1 to 2021 but he missed the space between each other, which number will be in the position 2021? And I though, why would I ever need to know that?
@unapersonaelegante4399  it's for developing your thinking and problem solving skills

@unapersonaelegante4399  The idea behind a question like that isn't that you need to know the answer to that question, it's so you can flex and strengthen your problem solving skills. Things that are useful and applicable outside of completely arbitrary math problems.

@unapersonaelegante4399  Isnt it fun tho? Taking problems as challenges. Lets see, after 9 1-digit numbers and 90 2-digit numbers we are at position 189, so the question is equivalent to asking what 3-digit number starting at 100 is at the position 2021-189=1832, so 1832/3 being 610th 3-digit number starting at 100, the answer should be 709. Is this correct? Anyways I just had fun, thanks for that

When mathematicians originally came up with this idea it was about decimals. Not hotels. And it's actually kinda important. It was the first crack in the idea that an infinite universe is infinitely knowable. It is mathematically impossible to account for every outcome. Before this it was assumed if you had the time, knew all the rules, and had all the information.. that you could solve the universe. But if it is mathematically impossible to account for every outcome, then it is mathematically impossible to solve the universe. It caused a huge problem in physics. A whole lot of people were angry at it. Eventually it lead to entire new fields of physics and mathematics like chaos theory and quantum mechanics. Things that did not try to give an exact answer, but an answer within a range.
High or low, mathematicians can always count on the numbered stuff they imagine. 👍
